reasoning: The National Financial Conditions Risk Subindex (NFCIRISK) currently sits noticeably below its long-term average of 0 (recent value -0.481, with expected value around April 12, 2026 being near -0.46). A value below zero indicates looser-than-average financial conditions. Over a long timeframe (e.g. 5-10 years), the index follows roughly a normal distribution centered at 0, meaning there is around a 65-68% chance that a random future draw will be higher than -0.46. In the short term, the index has exhibited an extremely strong upwards momentum over the past 11 weeks, climbing steadily from -0.65 to -0.48 without any weekly pullbacks. Given this steep ascent and strong local momentum, the likelihood of a continued increase in the very short horizon (1 week to 1 month) is quite high (approx 75-85%). As the horizon extends, uncertainty grows as economic conditions and Fed policy may shift to looser stances again in different business cycles. Thus, probability decays smoothly towards 0.5 from the peak short-term estimate, respecting both the long-term reversion to the mean and the provided instructions to increase uncertainty at longer horizons.
